title: Popowia lucidula
taxonomy: Popowia lucidula is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. It is native to New Caledonia. The Annonaceae family, also known as the custard-apple family, is a group of flowering plants that are primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. Popowia lucidula is part of the Annona genus-like section of the Popowia genus within the Annonaceae family. The classification of Popowia lucidula reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.

intro: Popowia lucidula is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the family Annonaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. Bioactive constituents of Popowia lucidula include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which have been identified as the primary compounds responsible for its medicinal properties.
